title: Mass layoffs at Xbox spark union protests, Carmack criticism, and debate about the company's future
summary: The layoffs at Xbox studios, including Bethesda and id Software, generate union marches, harsh statements from John Carmack, racist conspiracy theories, and intense analysis about the company's strategy, while Asha Sharma joins a Fed working group.

title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced crushes sales but is battered by its microtransactions
summary: The remake of the pirate classic sells two million copies in one day and sets a record on Steam, although microtransactions generate a wave of negative reviews and the company lays off workers from its Barcelona studio.

title: Media outlets recommend their film, series, and video game releases for the weekend
summary: Polygon, Eurogamer, 3DJuegos, Rock Paper Shotgun, and GameSpot publish their weekly guides with the best of streaming, cinemas, and video games, including free Game Pass titles.

title: Robert Yang denounces Steam censorship against his collection of experimental games 'Gay As A Service'
summary: The independent developer harshly criticizes Valve's moderation policies, which prevent his free bundle from being found in the store, in a controversy that has united several industry media outlets.

title: Pokémon Go celebrates its tenth anniversary with a massive Mewtwo raid in Times Square
summary: Niantic commemorates a decade of the game with a week of global events, including a recreation of the original trailer and new special research for trainers.

title: GTA Online announces the Kortz Center heist while anticipation for GTA 6 grows
summary: Rockstar details the first features of the new heist in GTA Online with an innovative replayability system, while the community waits for news about the multiplayer of the next installment.

title: Steam Machine returns with verification badges, e-ink covers, and the new Steam Frame format
summary: Valve prepares to launch the next-generation Steam Machines, with the 'Great on Frame' category and 'Steam Machine Verified' certification appearing on game pages.

title: Fire Emblem: Fortune's Weave and Atelier Yumia lead the new games for Nintendo Switch 2
summary: Details of the great Switch 2 strategic RPG are revealed, Atelier Yumia receives a performance update, and an indie game scheduled for 2025 is delayed to seek its viability on the new console.

title: The physical format on PlayStation wavers between boycott, the EU, and the confirmation of God of War Laufey on disc
summary: The 'No Disc, No Buy' boycott deflates with the Black Ops ports, the EU says it cannot force Sony, and God of War Laufey bets big on disc with its release scheduled for 2027.

title: Palworld redesigns its most controversial creatures and makes an economic decision that pleases players
summary: The studio Pocketpair modifies the designs that resembled Pokémon for its version 1.0 and announces a change in monetization that eases the burden on users' wallets.

title: Marvel Tōkon and Marvel Rivals mark the pulse of Marvel universe fighting games
summary: The first details of the new Marvel Tōkon are revealed, while the Marvel Rivals community debates whether the game should prioritize competitive balance or long-term fun.

title: GameStop Australia infuriates Pokémon TCG collectors with a sudden price increase
summary: GameStop Australian stores apply a massive price increase to Pokémon card packs, generating strong outrage among customers and the community.

title: Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth's Chocobos continue to terrify players with glitches two years after launch
summary: The peculiar behavior of the Chocobos in the Square Enix game continues to be a source of bugs and viral moments without patches having completely solved the problem.

title: Minecraft finally adds a key feature that players had been improvising for 17 years
summary: Mojang introduces a highly demanded mechanic that will allow constructions and processes that previously required tricks or external mods, opening a new world of creative possibilities.

title: Blizzard defends its most controversial decision in World of Warcraft and assures it is here to stay
summary: The developer states that the controversial change to the MMO's gameplay or economy, which has angered many veterans, is necessary for the game's long-term future.

title: Naruto confirms a live-action film with the director of Spider-Man: Brand New Day
summary: The popular anime and manga franchise follows in the footsteps of One Piece and announces the development of a live-action adaptation, generating enormous excitement.

title: Netflix threatens to take over film social network Letterboxd to expand its dominance
summary: According to a report, the streaming platform is considering acquiring the popular movie review and recommendation app.

title: The new port of Call of Duty Black Ops 2 retains the controversial emblem editor, which is flooded with swastikas
summary: Fans discover that the updated version of the classic includes the customization tool without filters, leading to mass creation of hate symbols.

title: Roku's horrible update enrages millions of users with its annoying interface changes
summary: The streaming platform applies a redesign that fills the screen with ads, hinders navigation, and removes popular features, causing a wave of complaints.

title: Thomas Mahler, creator of No Rest for the Wicked, criticizes the lack of innovation in the souls-like genre
summary: The well-known director of games like Ori says the industry has become lazy and that most games in the genre are limited to copying what already works without taking risks.
